THE ROOF OF ANOTHER TWO STORY HOME WAS TORN OFF ALONG HIGHWAY 8, AND A PORTABLE BUILDING WAS BLOWN NEARLY 400 YARDS INTO SOME NEARBY WOODS. THE TORNADO LIFTED AROUND 831 PM NEAR CR 2328 AND HIGHWAY 43 NORTH OF BIVINS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 8 MILES, WITH A WIDTH OF 190 YARDS.

NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DAMAGED EITHER BY FALLING TREES OR SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE FROM WIND ALONG US 71. THIS INCLUDES A BARN THAT WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. A EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 85-90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ALONG PR 4461 OR BUCK MOORE RD. THIS TORNADO REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR 6 MILES AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 2 AND OVER THE NORTHWEST SECTIONS OF LAKE DARBONNE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED, WITH THE GREATEST DAMAGE FOUND ON WALNUT TREE RD, WHERE SOME SHINGLES WERE REMOVED FROM A HOME, SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED, AND A WOODEN SHOP WAS MOVED OFF ITS FOUNDATION. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 826 AM ON PR 6606 OR ZION HILL RD. MAX. WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS.

*** 1 INJ *** WATER RUNNING OVER SALINE RIDGE ROAD SWEPT A TRUCK OFF THE ROAD. A HIGH WATER RESCUE WAS NEEDED FOR AN ELDERLY GENTLEMAN TRAPPED IN THE TRUCK. MAN WAS TAKEN TO THE HOSPITAL FOR HYPOTHERMIA...BUT IS NOW IN STABLE CONDITION.
